title: Accessible Button Roles
description:
Fix a set of **interactive elements** that are built with `<div>` and `<span>` tags instead of semantic HTML, making them inaccessible to keyboard users and screen readers.

### Requirements

- Convert clickable `<div>`s into proper `<button>` elements OR add `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and keyboard event handlers
- Ensure all interactive elements are keyboard-focusable (Tab key)
- Ensure all interactive elements respond to Enter and Space key presses
- Add appropriate `aria-label` or visible text for elements with only icon content
- A toggle button must use `aria-pressed` to indicate its state
- An icon-only button must have `aria-label` for screen reader text

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Action button | `action-btn` |
| Toggle button | `toggle-btn` |
| Icon button | `icon-btn` |
| Delete button | `delete-btn` |
| Output area | `output` |
constraints:
- Each interactive element must be keyboard operable (Enter + Space)
- Each element must have an accessible name
- Toggle button must communicate state via aria-pressed
- Icon-only buttons must have aria-label
- All elements must be focusable via Tab
hints: The simplest fix: replace <div onClick={...}> with <button onClick={...}>. Buttons are natively keyboard-accessible.
If you keep a <div>, add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler that triggers the click on Enter/Space.
For the toggle, set aria-pressed={isToggled} — the value must be a boolean, not a string.
